    }\n185\t          return c.json({ userAgent });\n186\t        });\n187\t\n188\t        // Note: Hono's Headers API automatically rejects CRLF in header values\n189\t        // This is handled at \"Mozilla/5.0\",\n193\t        });\n194\t\n195\t        const response = await app.request(\"/api/data\", {\n196\t          headers: validHeaders,\n197\t        });\n198\t\n199\t operations\", async () => {\n206\t      const app = new Hono();\n207\t\n208\t      const validToken = createMockCsrfToken().token;\n209\t\n210\t      app.post(\"/api/favorites\", (c) => {\n211\t }\n215\t        return c.json({ success: true });\n216\t      });\n217\t\n218\t      // Request without CSRF token should fail\n219\t      const responseWithoutToken = await expect(responseWithValidToken.status).toBe(200);\n242\t    });\n243\t\n244\t    it(\"should allow CSRF token validation\", () => {\n245\t      const token = createMockCsrfToken();\n246\t\n247\t      expect(token.token).toBeDefined();\n248\t      expect(token.token.length).toBeGreaterThan(16);\n249\t      expect(token.expiresAt).toBeGreaterThan(Date.now());\n250\t    });\n251\t  });\n252\t\n253\t  describe(\"Rate Limiting\", () => {\n254\t    it(\"should enforce rate limits\", async () => {\n255\t      const app = new Hono();\n256\t\n257\t      const requestCounts = new Map<string, number>();\n258\t      const MAX_REQUESTS = 5;\n259\t\n260\t      app.get(\"/api/arrivals\", (c) => {\n261\t        const ip = c.req.header(\"x-forwarded-for\") ?? \"unknown\";\n262\t        const count = requestCounts.get(ip) ?? 0;\n263\t\n264\t        if (count >= MAX_REQUESTS) {\n265\t          return c.json({ error: \"Rate limit exceeded\" }, 429);\n266\t        }\n267\t\n268\t        requestCounts.set(ip, count + 1);\n269\t        return c.json({ arrivals: [] });\n270\t      });\n271\t\n272\t      // First 5 requests should succeed\n273\t      for (let i = 0; i < MAX_REQUESTS; i++) {\n274\t        const response = await app.request(\"/api/arrivals\");\n275\t        expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n276\t      }\n277\t\n278\t      // 6th request should be rate limited\n279\t      const response = await app.request(\"/api/arrivals\");\n280\t      expect(response.status).toBe(429);\n281\t\n282\t      // Check rate limit headers\n283\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"Retry-After\")).toBeDefined();\n284\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Limit\")).toBeDefined();\n285\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\")).toBeDefined();\n286\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Reset\")).toBeDefined();\n287\t    });\n288\t\n289\t    it(\"should reset rate limits after time window\", async () => {\n290\t      const requestCounts = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();\n291\t      const WINDOW_MS = 60000;\n292\t      const MAX_REQUESTS = 5;\n293\t\n294\t      const checkRateLimit = (ip: string): boolean => {\n295\t        const now = Date.now();\n296\t        const state = requestCounts.get(ip);\n297\t\n298\t        if (!state || now > state.resetAt) {\n299\t          requestCounts.set(ip, { count: 1, resetAt: now + WINDOW_MS });\n300\t          return true;\n301\t        }\n302\t\n303\t        if (state.count >= MAX_REQUESTS) {\n304\t          return false;\n305\t        }\n306\t\n307\t        state.count++;\n308\t        return true;\n309\t      };\n310\t\n311\t      const ip = \"127.0.0.1\";\n312\t\n313\t      // Use all requests in the window\n314\t      for (let i = 0; i < MAX_REQUESTS; i++) {\n315\t        expect(checkRateLimit(ip)).toBe(true);\n316\t      }\n317\t\n318\t      // Next request should be rate limited\n319\t      expect(checkRateLimit(ip)).toBe(false);\n320\t\n321\t      // Simulate time window passing\n322\t      const state = requestCounts.get(ip)!;\n323\t      state.resetAt = Date.now() - 1000;\n324\t\n325\t      // Should be allowed again\n326\t      expect(checkRateLimit(ip)).toBe(true);\n327\t    });\n328\t  });\n329\t\n330\t  describe(\"Security Headers\", () => {\n331\t    it(\"should include all required security headers\", async () => {\n332\t      const app = new Hono();\n333\t\n334\t      app.use(\"*\", async (c, next) => {\n335\t        await  });\n346\t\n347\t      app.get(\"/api/data\", (c) => c.json({ data: \"test\" }));\n348\t\n349\t      const response = await app.request(\"/api/data\");\n350\t\n351\t expect(response.headers.get(\"X-XSS-Protection\")).toBe(\"1; mode=block\");\n354\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"Strict-Transport-Security\")).toContain(\"max-age=\");\n355\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"Content-Security-Policy\")).toContain(\"default-src\");\n356\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"Referrer-Policy\")).toBe(\"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\");\n357\t      expect(response.headers.get(\"Permissions-Policy\")).toContain(\"geolocation=()\");\n358\t    });\n359\t  });\n360\t\n361\t  describe(\"Authentication and Authorization\", () => {\n362\t => {\n438\t    it(\"should redact sensitive data from logs\", () => {\n439\t      const sensitiveData = {\n440\t        username: \"testuser\",\n441\t        password: \"secret123\",\n442\t        email: \"test@example.com\",\n443\t        apiKey: \"sk_test_12345\",\n444\t      };\n445\t\n446\t      const redactSensitive = (obj: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> => {\n447\t        const SENSITIVE_KEYS = [\"password\", \"passwd\", \"token\", \"secret\", \"apikey\", \"api_key\"];\n448\t\n449\t        const result: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n450\t        for (const [key, * Cross-cutting security test suite.\n *\n * Comprehensive security tests covering:\n * - Input validation and sanitization\n * - SQL injection prevention\n * - XSS prevention\n * - CSRF protection\n {\n      it(\"should detect SQL injection patterns\", () => {\n        for (const pattern of MALICIOUS_INPUTS.sqlInjection) {\n          expect(containsMaliciousPatterns(pattern)).toBe(true);\n        }\n      });\n\n      it(\"should sanitize SQL injection attempts\", () => {\n        const malicious = \"'; DROP TABLE users; --\";\n        const sanitized = sanitizeInput(malicious);\n\n        expect(sanitized).not.toContain(\"DROP TABLE\");\n        expect(sanitized).not.toContain(\";\");\n        expect(sanitized).not.toContain(\"'\");\n      });\n\n      it(\"should block SQL injection in API parameters\", async () => {\n        const app = new Hono();\n\n        app.get(\"/api/stations\", (c) => {\n          const stationId = c.req.query(\"stationId\");\n          if (containsMaliciousPatterns(stationId ?? \"\")) {\n            return c.json({ error: \"Invalid input\" }, 400);\n          }\n          return c.json({ stationId });\n        });\n\n        const maliciousPatterns = [\"1' OR '1'='1\", \"admin'--\", \"1' UNION SELECT * FROM users--\"];\n\n        for (const pattern of maliciousPatterns) {\n          const response = await app.request(\n            `/api/stations?stationId=${encodeURIComponent(pattern)}`\n          );\n          expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n        }\n      });\n    });\n\n    describe(\"XSS Prevention\", () => {\n     });\n\n      it(\"should escape HTML entities in API responses\", async () => {\n        const app = new Hono();\n\n        app.get(\"/api/stations\", (c) => {\n          const search =     const data = await response.json();\n\n        // In production, this should be escaped by the response handler\n        // For now, we're testing that the input doesn't break the API\n        expect(data.stations).toBeDefined();\n      });\n    });\n\n    describe(\"Path Traversal Prevention\", () => {\n      it(\"should detect path traversal patterns\", () => {\n        for (const pattern   });\n    });\n\n    describe(\"Command Injection Prevention\", () => {\n      it(\"should detect command injection patterns\", () => {\n        for (const pattern of {\n        const malicious = \"value\\r\\nX-Injected: true\";\n        const sanitized = sanitizeInput(malicious);\n\n        expect(sanitized).not.toContain(\"\\r\\n\");\n        expect(sanitized).not.toContain(\"\\n\");\n        expect(sanitized).not.toContain(\"\\r\");\n      });\n\n      it(\"should strip CRLF characters from headers\", async () => {\n        const app =            return c.json({ error: \"Invalid header\" }, 400);\n          }\n          return c.json({ userAgent });\n        });\n\n        // Note: Hono's Headers API automatically rejects CRLF in header values\n        // This is handled at the browser/HTTP library level\n        // In production, we should also validate on the server side\n        const validHeaders = new Headers({\n expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n      });\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"CSRF Protection\", () => {\n    it(\"should require CSRF token for state-changing operations\", async () => {\n      const () => {\n    it(\"should enforce rate limits\", async () => {\n      const app = new Hono();\n\n      const requestCounts = new Map<string, number>();\n      const MAX_REQUESTS = 5;\n\n      app.get(\"/api/arrivals\", (c) => {\n        const ip = c.req.header(\"x-forwarded-for\") ?? \"unknown\";\n        const count = requestCounts.get(ip) ?? 0;\n\n        if (count >= MAX_REQUESTS) {\n          return c.json({ error: \"Rate limit exceeded\" }, 429);\n        }\n\n        requestCounts.set(ip, count + 1);\n        return c.json({ arrivals: [] });\n      });\n\n      // First 5 requests should succeed\n      for (let i = 0; i < MAX_REQUESTS; i++) {\n        const response = await app.request(\"/api/arrivals\");\n        expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n      }\n\n      // 6th request should be rate limited\n      const response = await app.request(\"/api/arrivals\");\n      expect(response.status).toBe(429);\n\n      // Check rate limit headers\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"Retry-After\")).toBeDefined();\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Limit\")).toBeDefined();\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\")).toBeDefined();\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Reset\")).toBeDefined();\n    });\n\n    it(\"should reset rate limits after time window\", async () => {\n      const requestCounts = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();\n      const WINDOW_MS = 60000;\n      const MAX_REQUESTS = 5;\n\n      const checkRateLimit = (ip: string): boolean => {\n        const now = Date.now();\n        const state = requestCounts.get(ip);\n\n        if (!state || now > state.resetAt) {\n          requestCounts.set(ip, { count: 1, resetAt: now + WINDOW_MS });\n          return true;\n        }\n\n        if (state.count >= MAX_REQUESTS) {\n          return false;\n        }\n\n        state.count++;\n        return true;\n      };\n\n      const ip = \"127.0.0.1\";\n\n      // Use all requests in the window\n      for (let i = 0; i < MAX_REQUESTS; i++) {\n        expect(checkRateLimit(ip)).toBe(true);\n      }\n\n      // Next request should be rate limited\n      expect(checkRateLimit(ip)).toBe(false);\n\n      // Simulate time window passing\n      const state = requestCounts.get(ip)!;\n      state.resetAt = Date.now() - 1000;\n\n      // Should be allowed again\n      expect(checkRateLimit(ip)).toBe(true);\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Security Headers\", () => {\n    it(\"should include all required security headers\", async () => {\n      const app = new Hono();\n\n      app.use(\"*\", async (c, next) => {\n        await next();\n\n   c.header(\"Strict-Transport-Security\", \"max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains\");\n        c.header(\"Content-Security-Policy\", \"default-src 'self'\");\n        c.header(\"Referrer-Policy\", \"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\");\n        c.header(\"Permissions-Policy\", \"geolocation=(), microphone=()\");\n      });\n\n      app.get(\"/api/data\", (c) => c.json({ data: \"test\" }));\n\n      const response = await app.request(\"/api/data\");\n\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-Content-Type-Options\")).toBe(\"nosniff\");\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-Frame-Options\")).toBe(\"DENY\");\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"X-XSS-Protection\")).toBe(\"1; mode=block\");\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"Strict-Transport-Security\")).toContain(\"max-age=\");\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"Content-Security-Policy\")).toContain(\"default-src\");\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"Referrer-Policy\")).toBe(\"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\");\n      expect(response.headers.get(\"Permissions-Policy\")).toContain(\"geolocation=()\");\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Authentication and Authorization\", () => {\n    it(\"should require authentication for protected endpoints\", async () => {\n      const app = new Hono();\n\n      const validApiKey = createMockApiKey();\n\n      app.get(\"/api/user/profile\", (c) => {\n        const apiKey = c.req.header(\"x-api-key\");\n\n        if (!apiKey || apiKey !== validApiKey.keyId) {\n          return c.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, 401);\n        }\n\n        return c.json({ profile: { id: \"user_123\" } });\n      });\n\n      // Request without API key should fail\n      const responseWithoutKey = await app.request(\"/api/user/profile\");\n      expect(responseWithoutKey.status).toBe(401);\n\n      // Request with invalid API key should fail\n      const responseWithInvalidKey = await app.request(\"/api/user/profile\", {\n        headers: {\n          \"x-api-key\": \"invalid_key\",\n        },\n      });\n      expect(responseWithInvalidKey.status).toBe(401);\n\n      // Request with valid API key should succeed\n      const responseWithValidKey = await app.request(\"/api/user/profile\", {\n        headers: {\n          \"x-api-key\": validApiKey.keyId,\n        },\n      });\n      expect(responseWithValidKey.status).toBe(200);\n    });\n\n    it(\"should check permissions for authorized operations\", [\"user_456\", [\"read:arrivals\"]], // No write permissions\n      ]);\n\n      app.post(\"/api/favorites\", (c) => {\n        const userId = c.req.header(\"x-user-id\") ?? \"\";\n        const Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> => {\n        const SENSITIVE_KEYS = [\"password\", \"passwd\", \"token\", \"secret\", \"apikey\", \"api_key\"];\n\n        const result: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n        for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(obj)) {\n          if (\n            SENSITIVE_KEYS.some((sensitive) => key.toLowerCase().includes(sensitive.toLowerCase()))\n          ) {\n            result[key] = \"[REDACTED]\";\n          } else {\n            result[key] = value;\n          }\n        }\n expect(redacted.email).toBe(\"test@example.com\");\n      expect(redacted.apiKey).toBe(\"[REDACTED]\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"should not log sensitive request data\", () => {\n      const logEntry = (data: unknown): string => {\n        return JSON.stringify(data);\n      };\n\n      const requestData = {\n        username: \"testuser\",\n        password: \"secret123\",\n      };\n\n      const log = logEntry(requestData);\n\n      // In production, the password should be redacted before logging\n      // This test verifies the concept\n      expect(log).toBeDefined();\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Password Security\", () => {\n    it(\"should enforce password complexity requirements\", () => {\n      const isStrongPassword = (password: string): boolean => {\n        // Minimum 8 characters\n        if (password.length < 8) return false;\n\n        // Must contain uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character\n        const hasUpperCase = /[A-Z]/.test(password);\n        const hasLowerCase = /[a-z]/.test(password);\n        const hasNumber = /[0-9]/.test(password);\n        const hasSpecial = /[^A-Za-z0-9]/.test(password);\n\n        return hasUpperCase && hasLowerCase && hasNumber && hasSpecial;\n      };\n\n      expect(isStrongPassword(\"weak\")).toBe(false);\n      expect(isStrongPassword(\"password123\")).toBe(false);\n      expect(isStrongPassword(\"Password123!\")).toBe(true);\n      expect(isStrongPassword(\"V3ry$tr0ng!P@ssw0rd#\")).toBe(true);\n    });\n\n    it(\"should hash passwords with appropriate algorithm\", () => {\n      const mockPassword = \"test_password_123\";\n\n      // In production, use argon2 or bcrypt\n      // This test verifies the concept\n      const hash = (password: string): string => {\n        return `$argon2id$v=19$m=65536,t=3,p=4$${password}`;\n      };\n\n      const hashed = hash(mockPassword);\n\n      expect(hashed).toBeDefined();\n      expect(hashed).not.toBe(mockPassword);\n      expect(hashed).toContain(\"argon2id\");\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"API Key Security\", () => {\n    it(\"should validate API key format\", () => {\n      const isValidApiKeyFormat = (key: return /^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$/.test(key);\n      };\n\n      expect(isValidApiKeyFormat(\"short\")).toBe(false);\n      expect(isValidApiKeyFormat(\"invalid@key!\")).toBe(false);\n    };\n\n      expect(isExpired(apiKey)).toBe(true);\n\n      const validKey = createMockApiKey({\n        expiresAt: Date.now() + 3600000, // Expires in 1 hour\n      });\n\n      expect(isExpired(validKey)).toBe(false);\n    });\n\n    it(\"should track failed authentication attempts\", () => {\n      const failedAttempts = new Map<string, number>();\n\n      const recordFailedAttempt = (keyId: string): void => {\n        const count = failedAttempts.get(keyId) ?? 0;\n        failedAttempts.set(keyId, count + 1);\n      };\n\n      const isKeyLocked = (keyId: string): boolean => {\n        return (failedAttempts.get(keyId) ?? 0) >= 3;\n      };\n\n      const keyId = \"key_test_123\";\n\n      expect(isKeyLocked(keyId)).toBe(false);\n\n * Security store persistence layer.\n *\n * Provides write-through SQLite persistence for the in-memory Maps used by:\n *   - api-key-management.ts  (API_KEY_REGISTRY, API_KEY_LAST_USED, API_KEY_DESCRIPTIONS)\n *   - password-management.ts (passwordResetTokens, passwordHistory, passwordResetAttempts, accountLockouts)\n *   - auth-rate-limit.ts     (rate limit bans, trusted IPs)\n *   - suspicious-activity-notifications.ts (preferences, events, history, templates)\n *\n * Usage:\n *   import { setSecurityDb } from './security/security-db.js';\n *   setSecurityDb(pushDb); // call once after runMigrations()\n *\n * All write functions are no-ops when the DB has not been initialised, so\n * existing unit-tests that never call setSecurityDb() continue to work.\n *\n * Types are defined inline (no imports from middleware modules) to avoid\n * circular-dependency issues — the middleware modules themselves import from\n * this file.\n */\n\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n\n// ── DB singleton ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nlet _db: Database.Database | null = null;\n\nexport function setSecurityDb(db: Database.Database): void {\n  _db = db;\n}\n\nexport function isSecurityDbReady(): boolean {\n  return _db !== null;\n}\n\nfunction db(): Database.Database {\n  if (!_db) throw new Error(\"Security DB not initialised — call setSecurityDb() first\");\n  return _db;\n}\n\n// ── Inline type shapes (mirror the interfaces in middleware modules) ──────────\n\nexport interface StoredApiKey {\n  keyId: string;\n  keyHash: string;\n  keySalt: string;\n  scope: string;\n  role?: string;\n  additionalPermissions?: string[];\n  owner?: string;\n  rateLimitTier: number;\n  active: boolean;\n  createdAt: number;\n  expiresAt: number;\n  failedAttempts: number;\n  lastFailedAt?: number;\n  lockedUntil?: number;\n}\n\nexport interface StoredApiKeyEntry {\n  key: StoredApiKey;\n  description?: string;\n  lastUsedAt?: number;\n}\n\nexport interface StoredPasswordResetToken {\n  tokenId: string;\n  keyId: string;\n  tokenHash: string;\n  createdAt: number;\n  expiresAt: number;\n  used: boolean;\n registry ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nexport function loadApiKeyRegistry(): StoredApiKeyEntry[] {\n  if (!_db) return [];\n  const rows = db().prepare(\"SELECT * FROM security_api_key_registry\").all() as Array<{\n    key_id: string;\n    key_hash: string;\n    key_salt: string;\n    scope: string;\n    role: string | null;\n    additional_permissions: string;\n locked_until: number | null;\n    description: string | null;\n    last_used_at: number | null;\n  }>;\n\n  return rows.map((r) => ({\n    key: {\n      keyId: r.key_id,\n      keyHash: r.key_hash,\n      keySalt: r.key_salt,\n      scope: r.scope,\n      role: r.role ?? undefined,\n      additionalPermissions: JSON.parse(r.additional_permissions) as string[],\n      owner: r.owner ?? undefined,\n r.last_failed_at ?? undefined,\n      lockedUntil: r.locked_until ?? undefined,\n    },\n    description: r.description ?? undefined,\n    lastUsedAt: r.last_used_at ?? undefined,\n  }));\n}\n\nexport function saveApiKey(key: StoredApiKey, description?: string, lastUsedAt?: number): void {\n  if (!_db) return;\n  db()\n    .prepare(\n      `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO security_api_key_registry\n       (key_id, key_hash, key_salt, scope, role, additional_permissions, owner,\n        rate_limit_tier, active, created_at, expires_at, failed_attempts,\n        last_failed_at, locked_until, description, JSON.stringify(key.additionalPermissions ?? []),\n      key.owner ?? null,\n      key.rateLimitTier,\n      key.active ? 1 : 0,\n      key.createdAt,\n      key.expiresAt,\n      key.failedAttempts,\n      key.lastFailedAt ?? null,\n      key.lockedUntil ?? null,\n      description ?? null,\n      lastUsedAt ?? null\n    );\n}\n\nexport function updateApiKeyLastUsed(keyId: string, lastUsedAt: number): void {\n  if (!_db) return;\n  db()\n    .prepare(\"UPDATE security_api_key_registry SET last_used_at = ? OR key LIKE ?\")\n    .run(email, `${email}:%`);\n}\n\n// ── Account lockouts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nexport function loadAccountLockouts(): Map<\n  string,\n  { lockedUntil: number; ALLOWED_HOSTS is set in production (prevents host header injection)\n * - PASSWORD_PEPPER is set (optional but recommended)\n * - VAPID keys are configured (required for push notifications)\n *\n * In development mode, only warnings are issued. In production mode,\n * critical misconfigurations will cause startup to fail.\n */\nexport function validateSecurityConfiguration(): ValidationResult {\n  const result: ValidationResult = {\n    passed: true,\n    warnings: [],\n    errors: [],\n  };\n\n  const isProduction = process.env[\"NODE_ENV\"] === \"production\";\n  const isTest = process.env[\"TEST_MODE\"] === \"true\";\n\n  // Skip validation in test mode\n  if (isTest) {\n    logger.debug(\"Security validation skipped in test mode\");\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // ALLOWED_HOSTS validation\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  const allowedHosts = process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"];\n  if (!allowedHosts || allowedHosts.trim() === \"\") {\n    const message =\n      \"ALLOWED_HOSTS is not set. This allows any hostname, which may expose the application to host header injection attacks.\";\n\n    if (isProduction) {\n      result.errors.push(message);\n      result.passed = false;\n    } else {\n      result.warnings.push(message + \" (OK for development)\");\n    }\n  }\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // PASSWORD_PEPPER validation\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  const passwordPepper = process.env[\"PASSWORD_PEPPER\"];\n  if (!passwordPepper || passwordPepper.trim() === \"\" || passwordPepper.length < 32) {\n    const message =\n      \"PASSWORD_PEPPER is not set or too short (should be at least 32 bytes / 64 hex chars). This reduces password security by removing a layer of defense.\";\n\n    result.warnings.push(message);\n  }\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // VAPID keys validation\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  const vapidPublicKey = process.env[\"VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY\"];\n  const vapidPrivateKey = process.env[\"VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY\"];\n\n  if (!vapidPublicKey || vapidPublicKey.trim() === \"\") {\n    const message = \"VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY is not set. Push notifications will not work.\";\n    result.warnings.push(message);\n  }\n\n  if (!vapidPrivateKey || vapidPrivateKey.trim() === \"\") {\n    const message = \"VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY is not set. Push notifications will not work.\";\n    result.warnings.push(message);\n  }\n\n  // Log warnings\n  if (result.warnings.length > 0) {\n    logger.warn(\"Security configuration warnings\", {\n      warnings: result.warnings,\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Log errors and fail in production\n  if (result.errors.length > 0) {\n    logger.error(\"Security configuration errors\", undefined, {\n      errors: result.errors,\n      fatal: true,\n    });\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate security configuration and throw if critical issues are found.\n *\n * Use this at startup to fail-fast on security misconfigurations.\n *\n * @throws Error if critical security issues are found in production\n */\nexport function validateSecurityOrThrow(): void {\n  const result = validateSecurityConfiguration();\n\n  if (!result.passed) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Security validation failed:\\n${result.errors.map((e) => `  - ${e}`).join(\"\\n\")}`\n    );\n  }\n}\n","numLines":129,"startLine":1,"totalLines":129}}}

}\n228\t\n229\t  const upperQuery = originalQuery.toUpperCase();\n230\t  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n231\t    return true;\n232\t  }\n233\t\n234\t  const expandedName = Supports brotli, gzip, and deflate based on Accept-Encoding header.\n290\t * Brotli is preferred for best compression ratio.\n291\t */\n292\tfunction compressionMiddleware(): MiddlewareHandler {\n293\t  return async (c, next) => {\n294\t    await next();\n295\t\n296\t    const body = c.res.body;\n297\t    if (!body || c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Encoding\")) return;\n298\t\n299\t    const contentType = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Type\");\n300\t    if (!contentType?.includes(\"json\") && !contentType?.includes(\"text\")) return;\n301\t\n302\t    const acceptEncoding = c.req.header(\"Accept-Encoding\") || \"\";\n303\t    const contentLength = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Length\");\n304\t\n305\t    // Skip small responses (not worth compressing)\n306\t    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) < 500) return;\n307\t\n308\t    // Clone response to get the body as buffer\n309\t    const buffer = Buffer.from(await c.res.clone().arrayBuffer());\n310\t\n311\t    // Prefer brotli for best compression, then gzip, then deflate\n312\t    if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"br\")) {\n313\t      const brotlied = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n314\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n315\t        const brotli = createBrotliCompress();\n316\t        brotli.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n317\t        brotli.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n318\t        brotli.on(\"error\", reject);\n319\t        brotli.end(buffer);\n320\t      });\n321\t\n322\t      c.res = new Response(brotlied, {\n323\t        status: c.res.status,\n324\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n325\t      });\n326\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"br\");\n327\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n328\t    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"gzip\")) {\n329\t      const gzipped = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n330\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n331\t        const gzip = createGzip();\n332\t        gzip.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n333\t        gzip.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n334\t        gzip.on(\"error\", reject);\n335\t        gzip.end(buffer);\n336\t      });\n337\t\n338\t      c.res = new Response(gzipped, {\n339\t        status: c.res.status,\n340\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n341\t      });\n342\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"gzip\");\n343\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n344\t    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"deflate\")) {\n345\t      const deflated = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n346\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n347\t        const deflate = createDeflate();\n348\t        deflate.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n349\t        deflate.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n350\t        deflate.on(\"error\", reject);\n351\t        deflate.end(buffer);\n352\t      });\n353\t\n354\t      c.res = new Response(deflated, {\n355\t        status: c.res.status,\n356\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n357\t      });\n358\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"deflate\");\n359\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n360\t    }\n361\t  };\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t/**\n365\t * Check if a path has a content hash (for immutable caching).\n366\t * Vite produces files like: assets/index-a1b2c3d4.js\n367\t */\n368\tfunction isHashedAsset(path: string): boolean {\n369\t  // Match pattern: /assets/name-[hash].ext where hash is 8+ hex chars\n370\t  return /\\/assets\\/[^/]+-[a-f0-9]{8,}\\.(js|css|svg|png|jpg|ico|woff2?)$/i.test(path);\n371\t}\n372\t\n373\texport function createApp(\n374\t  stations: StationIndex,\n375\t  routes: RouteIndex,\n376\t  complexes: ComplexIndex,\n377\t  transfers: Record<string, TransferConnection[]>,\n378\t  webDistPath: string\n379\t): Hono {\n380\t  const app = new Hono();\n381\t\n382\t  // Request ID for correlation across logs and audit events.\n383\t  // Must run before security logging and audit middleware.\n384\t  app.use(\"*\", requestId);\n385\t\n386\t  // Security headers on all responses (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)\n387\t  // CSP includes report-uri for violation monitoring at /api/security/csp-report\n388\t  app.use(\n389\t    \"*\",\n390\t    securityHeaders({\n391\t      reportUri: \"/api/security/csp-report\",\n392\t    })\n393\t  );\n394\t\n395\t  // Security logging for all requests (OWASP A09: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures)\n396\t  // Logs authentication failures, authorization failures, rate limit exceeded, and blocked attacks\n397\t app.use(\"*\", httpMethodRestrictions());\n402\t\n403\t  // HTTP Request Smuggling protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n404\t  // Detects and blocks request smuggling attempts\n405\t  app.use(\"*\", httpRequestSmuggling());\n406\t\n407\t  // HTTP Response Splitting protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n408\t  // Detects and blocks CRLF injection attempts\n409\t  app.use(\"*\", httpResponseSplitting());\n410\t\n411\t  // Host header protection to prevent cache poisoning and password reset poisoning\n412\t  // In production, set ALLOWED_HOSTS environment variable to restrict allowed hosts\n413\t  const isProduction = process.env[\"NODE_ENV\"] === \"production\";\n414\t  const allowedHosts = process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"]\n415\t    ? process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"].split(\",\")\n416\t    : undefined;\n417\t  app.use(\n418\t    \"*\",\n419\t    hostHeaderProtection({\n420\t      allowedHosts,\n421\t      blockMissingHost: isProduction, // Only require Host header in production\n422\t      blockIpAddresses: isProduction,\n423\t      blockPrivateNetworks: isProduction,\n424\t      blockLocalhost: isProduction, // Block routes (DoS protection)\n432\t  app.use(\"*\", requestSizeLimits());\n433\t\n434\t  // Input sanitization for all API routes (XSS, SQL injection prevention)\n435\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", inputSanitization());\n436\t\n437\t  // JSON depth protection for API routes (OWASP A01, A03)\n438\t  // Prevents DoS via deeply nested JSON structures\n439\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", jsonDepthProtection());\n440\t\n441\t  // Optional authentication for all API routes\n442\t  // Parses Authorization header and sets auth context if present\n443\t  // Individual routes can require specific permissions using requirePermission\n444\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", optionalAuth({ allowSessions: true }));\n445\t\n446\t  // CSRF protection for state-changing operations\n447\t  // Excludes health, metrics, and safe read-only endpoints\n448\t  // NOTE: /status is a public read-only HTML page (not under /api/*, so not affected)\n449\t  // NOTE: /api/context, /api/auth/oauth, /api/auth/mfa, /api/auth/session are disabled\n450\t  // NOTE: /api/auth/password is enabled for password reset functionality\n451\t  app.use(\n452\t    \"/api/*\",\n453\t    csrfProtection({\n454\t      excludePaths: [\n455\t        \"/api/health\",\n456\t        \"/api/metrics\",\n457\t        \"/api/stations\",\n458\t        \"/api/routes\",\n459\t        \"/api/static\",\n460\t \"/api/auth/mfa\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n470\t        // \"/api/auth/session\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n471\t        \"/api/auth/password\",\n472\t        \"/api/csrf-token\",\n473\t        \"/api/security/csp-report\", // CSP violation reports (browser-initiated)\n474\t      ],\n475\t    })\n476\t  );\n477\t\n478\t  // HPP protection for all API routes (prevents parameter pollution attacks)\n479\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", hppProtection({ strategy: \"first\" }));\n480\t\n481\t  // HTTP metrics collection for all API routes\n482\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", httpMetrics());\n483\t\n484\t  // Rate limiting on all API routes (60 req/min per IP, token bucket)\n485\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", rateLimiter());\n486\t\n487\t  // Response size limits for API routes (OWASP A01, A04)\n488\t  // Prevents DoS via large response payloads\n489\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", responseSizeLimits());\n490\t\n491\t  // Compression for API responses\n492\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", compressionMiddleware());\n493\t\n494\t  const stationToComplex = buildStationToComplexMap(complexes);\n495\t\n496\t  // Create transfer engine for commute analysis\n497\t  const transferEngine = createTransferEngine({\n498\t    stations,\n499\t    routes,\n500\t    transfers,\n501\t    complexes,\n502\t    getArrivals: (stationId: string) => {\n503\t      return getArrivals(stationId);\n504\t    },\n505\t  });\n506\t\n507\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n508\t  // CSRF token endpoint\n509\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n510\t  app.get(\n511\t    \"/api/csrf-token\",\n512\t    cors({\n513\t      allowedOrigins: [\"*\"], // Allow all origins for function timeAgo(isoString: string | null): string {\n528\t    if (!isoString) return \"Never\";\n529\t    const ms = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();\n530\t    const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n531\t    if (seconds < 60) return `${seconds}s ago`;\n532\t    const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);\n533\t    if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago`;\n534\t    const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);\n535\t    if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago`;\n536\t    const days = Math.floor(hours / 24);\n537\t    return `${days}d ago`;\n538\t  }\n539\t\n540\t  /**\n541\t   * Format bytes as human-readable (e.g., \"45.2 MB\")\n542\t   */\n543\t  function formatBytes(bytes: number): string {\n544\t    const units = [\"B\", \"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"];\n545\t    let i = 0;\n546\t    while (bytes >= 1024 && i < units.length - 1) {\n547\t      bytes /= 1024;\n548\t      i++;\n549\t    }\n550\t    return `${bytes.toFixed(1)} ${units[i]}`;\n551\t  }\n552\t\n553\t    <a href=\"/api/health\" style=\"color: #38bdf8;\">View JSON</a>\n865\t    </div>\n866\t  </div>\n867\t</body>\n868\t</html>`;\n869\t  }\n870\t\n871\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n872\t  // Health endpoint\n873\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n874\t  /**\n875\t   * GET /api/health — Per-feed health status (JSON)\n876\t   *\n877\t   * Query validation: Uses `emptyQuerySchema` (`z.object({}).strict()`).\n878\t   * Any query parameters at all cause a 400 with `{ error: \"validation failed\", details: [...] }`.\n879\t   * No query parameters are accepted.\n880\t   *\n881\t   * Response top-level fields:\n882\t   *   status              (\"ok\" | \"degraded\")\n883\t   *     — \"ok\" when all feeds have circuitOpenAt===null AND lastSuccessAt!==null AND !isStale,\n884\t   *       AND alerts has circuitOpen===false AND lastSuccessAt!==null.\n885\t   *     — \"degraded\" otherwise.\n886\t   *\n887\t   *   timestamp           string (ISO 8601) — new Date().toISOString() at request time.\n888\t   *\n889\t   *   uptime_seconds      number — seconds since SERVER_START_MS (set at module load).\n890\t   *\n891\t   *   feeds               Array<FeedState> — one entry per GTFS-realtime feed.\n892\t   *     Each element:\n893\t   *       id                    string   — feed identifier (e.g. \"1\", \"26\", \"31\", \"36\").\n894\t   *       name                  string   — human-readable feed name.\n895\t   *       status                (\"circuit_open\" | \"never_polled\" | \"stale\" | \"ok\")\n896\t   *         — \"circuit_open\" when circuitOpenAt !== null.\n897\t   *         — \"never_polled\" when lastSuccessAt === null.\n898\t   *         — \"stale\" when isStale === true.\n899\t   *         — \"ok\" otherwise.\n900\t   *       lastSuccessAt         string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful poll, or null.\n901\t   *       lastPollAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last poll attempt, or null.\n902\t   *       consecutiveFailures  number — count of consecutive failed polls.\n903\t   *       entityCount           number — number of entities in the feed.\n904\t   *       lastError             string | null — truncated (max 100 chars) last error message,\n905\t   *                                  newlines stripped; null if no error.\n906\t   *       tripReplacementPeriod string | null — the feed's trip replacement period.\n907\t   *       avgLatencyMs          number — average of latencyHistory entries.\n908\t   *       errorCount24h         number — count of error timestamps within the last 24 hours.\n909\t   *       parseErrors           (feed-specific type) — parse error details for the feed.\n910\t   *\n911\t   *   alerts              object — alerts polling subsystem status.\n912\t   *     count                  number  — number of alerts tracked.\n913\t   * of active push notification subscriptions.\n939\t   *\n940\t   *   cacheHitRate        number — fraction 0–1, computed as cache_hits_total /\n941\t   *                            (cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total) summed across all\n942\t   *                            label combinations. *   200 — when fewer than UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD (3) feeds are failing\n956\t   *         (consecutiveFailures > 0, had prior success, last success > 5 min ago).\n957\t   *   503 — when 3 or more authentication functionality.\n// import {\n//   cleanupExpiredStates,\n//   createAuthorizationUrl,\n//   getActiveOAuthProviders,\n//   handleOAuthCallback,\n//   initializeDefaultProviders,\n// } from \"./oauth/index.js\";\nimport { logger } from \"./observability/logger.js\";\nimport { metrics } from \"./observability/metrics.js\";\nimport { tracingMiddleware } from \"./observability/tracing.js\";\nimport { buildLineDiagram } from \"./positions-interpolator.js\";\nimport {\n  getSubscriptionCount,\n  getSubscriptionOwner,\n  removeSubscription,\n  updateSubscriptionFavorites,\n  updateSubscriptionMorningScores,\n  updateSubscriptionQuietHours,\n  upsertSubscription,\n} from \"./push/subscriptions.js\";\nimport { getVapidPublicKey } from \"./push/vapid.js\";\nimport { buildPasswordResetRoutes } from \"./routes/password-reset.routes.js\";\nimport { createTransferEngine } from \"./transfer/index.js\";\nimport { lookupTrip } from \"./trip-lookup.js\";\nimport {\n  calculateCommuteStats,\n  deleteTrip,\n  getTotalTripCount,\n  getTripById,\n  getTrips,\n  recordTrip,\n  updateTripNotes,\n} from \"./trip-tracking.js\";\n\n/** Server start time for uptime calculation */\nconst SERVER_START_MS = Date.now();\n\n/** Number of feeds failing >5 min before returning 503 */\nconst UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD = 3;\n\n/** Cache header for static GTFS data */\nconst STATIC_CACHE_HEADER = `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.gtfsStatic}, stale-while-revalidate=${CACHE_TTLS.gtfsStaticStale}`;\n\n/**\n * Common abbreviation mappings for station search\n */\nconst ABBREVIATIONS: Record<string, string> = {\n  sq: \"square\",\n  st: \"street\",\n  ave: \"avenue\",\n  av: \"avenue\",\n  blvd: \"boulevard\",\n  pkwy: \"parkway\",\n  rd: \"road\",\n  dr: \"drive\",\n  ln: \"lane\",\n  ct: \"court\",\n  pl: \"place\",\n  hwy: \"highway\",\n  expwy: \"expressway\",\n  bway: \"broadway\",\n  \"'way\": \"way\",\n};\n\nfunction expandAbbreviations(term: string): string {\n  const lower = term.toLowerCase();\n  if (ABBREVIATIONS[lower]) {\n    return ABBREVIATIONS[lower];\n  }\n  let expanded = term;\n  for (const [abbr, full] of Object.entries(ABBREVIATIONS)) {\n    const regex = new RegExp(`\\\\b${abbr}\\\\b`, \"gi\");\n    expanded = expanded.replace(regex, full);\n  }\n  return expanded;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeForSearch(str: string): string {\n  return str\n    .toLowerCase()\n    .replace(/[^a-z0-9\\s]/g, \"\")\n    .replace(/\\s+/g, \" \")\n    .trim();\n}\n\nfunction stationMatchesQuery(\n  station: Station,\n  normalizedQuery: string,\n  originalQuery: string\n): boolean {\n  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n  const expandedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(originalQuery));\n  const expandedNormalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(normalizedQuery));\n\n  if (\n    normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery) ||\n    normalizedName.includes(expandedQuery) ||\n    normalizedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)\n  ) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const upperQuery = originalQuery.toUpperCase();\n  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const expandedName = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(station.name));\n  if (expandedName.includes(expandedQuery) || expandedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\nfunction scoreSearchResult(station: Station, query: string): number {\n  const normalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(query);\n  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n  const upperQuery = query.toUpperCase();\n\n  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n    return 1000;\n  }\n\n  if (normalizedName.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {\n    return 100;\n  }\n\n  const words = normalizedName.split(/\\s+/);\n  if (words.some((word) => word.startsWith(normalizedQuery))) {\n    return 50;\n  }\n\n  if (normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery)) {\n    return 10;\n  }\n\n  return 1;\n}\n\nfunction buildStationToComplexMap(complexes: ComplexIndex): Map<string, StationComplex> {\n  const map = new Map<string, StationComplex>();\n  for (const complex of Object.values(complexes)) {\n    for (const stationId of complex.stations) {\n      map.set(stationId, complex);\n    }\n  }\n  return map;\n}\n\n/**\n * @param stations         Pre-loaded GTFS static station index\n * @param routes           Pre-loaded GTFS static route index\n * @param complexes        Pre-loaded station complexes index\n * @param transfers        Pre-loaded transfer connections\n * @param webDistPath      Absolute path to the built React PWA (packages/web/dist)\n */\n/** Cache header for immutable assets (hashed filenames) */\nconst IMMUTABLE_CACHE_HEADER = \"public, max-age=31536000, immutable\";\n\n/**\n * Compression middleware for API responses.\n * Supports brotli, gzip, and deflate based on Accept-Encoding header.\n * Brotli is preferred for best compression ratio.\n */\nfunction compressionMiddleware(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    await next();\n\n    const body = c.res.body;\n    if (!body || c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Encoding\")) return;\n\n    const contentType = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Type\");\n    if (!contentType?.includes(\"json\") && !contentType?.includes(\"text\")) return;\n\n    const acceptEncoding = c.req.header(\"Accept-Encoding\") || \"\";\n    const contentLength = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Length\");\n\n    // Skip small responses (not worth compressing)\n    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) < 500) return;\n\n    // Clone response to get the body as buffer\n    const buffer = Buffer.from(await c.res.clone().arrayBuffer());\n\n    // Prefer brotli for best compression, then gzip, then deflate\n    if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"br\")) {\n      const brotlied = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const brotli = createBrotliCompress();\n        brotli.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        brotli.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        brotli.on(\"error\", reject);\n        brotli.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(brotlied, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"br\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"gzip\")) {\n      const gzipped = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const gzip = createGzip();\n        gzip.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        gzip.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        gzip.on(\"error\", reject);\n        gzip.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(gzipped, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"gzip\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"deflate\")) {\n      const deflated = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const deflate = createDeflate();\n        deflate.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        deflate.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        deflate.on(\"error\", reject);\n        deflate.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(deflated, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"deflate\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    }\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a path has a content hash (for immutable caching).\n * Vite produces files like: assets/index-a1b2c3d4.js\n */\nfunction isHashedAsset(path: string): boolean {\n  // Match pattern: /assets/name-[hash].ext where hash is 8+ hex chars\n  return /\\/assets\\/[^/]+-[a-f0-9]{8,}\\.(js|css|svg|png|jpg|ico|woff2?)$/i.test(path);\n}\n\nexport function createApp(\n  stations: StationIndex,\n  routes: RouteIndex,\n  complexes: ComplexIndex,\n  transfers: Record<string, TransferConnection[]>,\n  webDistPath: string\n): Hono {\n  const app = new Hono();\n\n  // Request ID for correlation across logs and audit events.\n  // Must run before security logging and audit middleware.\n  app.use(\"*\", requestId);\n\n  // Security headers on all responses (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)\n  // CSP includes report-uri for violation monitoring at /api/security/csp-report\n  app.use(\n    \"*\",\n    securityHeaders({\n      reportUri: \"/api/security/csp-report\",\n    })\n  );\n\n  // Security logging for all requests (OWASP A09: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures)\n  // Logs authentication failures, authorization failures, rate limit exceeded, and blocked attacks\n  app.use(\"*\", securityLogging());\n\n  // HTTP method restrictions (OWASP A01, A05)\n  // Blocks dangerous HTTP methods (TRACE, CONNECT) that could be used for attacks\n  app.use(\"*\", httpMethodRestrictions());\n\n  // HTTP Request Smuggling protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Detects and blocks request smuggling attempts\n  app.use(\"*\", httpRequestSmuggling());\n\n  // HTTP Response Splitting protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Detects and blocks CRLF injection attempts\n  app.use(\"*\", httpResponseSplitting());\n\n  // Host header protection to prevent cache poisoning and password reset poisoning\n  // In production, set ALLOWED_HOSTS environment variable to restrict allowed hosts\n  const isProduction = process.env[\"NODE_ENV\"] === \"production\";\n  const allowedHosts = process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"]\n    ? process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"].split(\",\")\n    : undefined;\n  app.use(\n    \"*\",\n    hostHeaderProtection({\n      allowedHosts,\n      blockMissingHost: isProduction, // Only require Host header in production\n      blockIpAddresses: isProduction,\n      blockPrivateNetworks: isProduction,\n      blockLocalhost: isProduction, // Block localhost requests in production\n    })\n  );\n\n  // Distributed tracing for all requests\n  app.use(\"*\", tracingMiddleware);\n\n  // Request size limits for all routes (DoS protection)\n  app.use(\"*\", requestSizeLimits());\n\n  // Input sanitization for all API routes (XSS, SQL injection prevention)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", inputSanitization());\n\n  // JSON depth protection for API routes (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Prevents DoS via deeply nested JSON structures\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", jsonDepthProtection());\n\n  // Optional authentication for all API routes\n  // Parses Authorization header and sets auth context if present\n  // Individual routes can require specific permissions using requirePermission\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", optionalAuth({ allowSessions: true }));\n\n  // CSRF protection for state-changing operations\n  // Excludes health, metrics, and safe read-only endpoints\n  // NOTE: /status is a public read-only HTML page (not under /api/*, so not affected)\n  // NOTE: /api/context, /api/auth/oauth, /api/auth/mfa, /api/auth/session are disabled\n  // NOTE: /api/auth/password is enabled for password reset functionality\n  app.use(\n    \"/api/*\",\n    csrfProtection({\n      excludePaths: [\n        \"/api/health\",\n        \"/api/metrics\",\n        \"/api/stations\",\n        \"/api/routes\",\n        \"/api/static\",\n        \"/api/arrivals\",\n        \"/api/alerts\",\n        \"/api/equipment\",\n        \"/api/trip\",\n        \"/api/positions\",\n        \"/api/push/vapid-public-key\",\n        \"/api/journal\",\n        // \"/api/context\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/oauth\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/mfa\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/session\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        \"/api/auth/password\",\n        \"/api/csrf-token\",\n        \"/api/security/csp-report\", // CSP violation reports (browser-initiated)\n      ],\n    })\n  );\n\n  // HPP protection for all API routes (prevents parameter pollution attacks)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", hppProtection({ strategy: \"first\" }));\n\n  // HTTP metrics collection for all API routes\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", httpMetrics());\n\n  // Rate limiting on all API routes (60 req/min per IP, token bucket)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", rateLimiter());\n\n  // Response size limits for API routes (OWASP A01, A04)\n  // Prevents DoS via large response payloads\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", responseSizeLimits());\n\n  // Compression for API responses\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", compressionMiddleware());\n\n  const stationToComplex = buildStationToComplexMap(complexes);\n\n  // Create transfer engine for commute analysis\n  const transferEngine = createTransferEngine({\n    stations,\n    routes,\n    transfers,\n    complexes,\n    getArrivals: (stationId: string) => {\n      return getArrivals(stationId);\n    },\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // CSRF token endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\n    \"/api/csrf-token\",\n    cors({\n      allowedOrigins: [\"*\"], // Allow all origins for CSRF token\n      allowedMethods: [\"GET\"],\n      allowedHeaders: [\"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"],\n    }),\n    (c) => {\n      const token = generateCsrfToken();\n\n      return c.json({ token });\n    }\n  );\n\n  /**\n   * Format a timestamp as \"X ago\" for human-readable display\n   */\n  function timeAgo(isoString: string | null): string {\n    if (!isoString) return \"Never\";\n    const ms = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();\n    const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n    if (seconds < 60) return `${seconds}s ago`;\n    const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);\n    if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago`;\n    const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);\n    if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago`;\n    const days = Math.floor(hours / 24);\n    return `${days}d ago`;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Format bytes as human-readable (e.g., \"45.2 MB\")\n   */\n  function formatBytes(bytes: number): string {\n    const units = [\"B\", \"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"];\n    let i = 0;\n    while (bytes >= 1024 && i < units.length - 1) {\n      bytes /= 1024;\n      i++;\n    }\n    return `${bytes.toFixed(1)} ${units[i]}`;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Build the HTML response for the /status dashboard\n   */\n  function buildStatusHtml(healthData: {\n    status: string;\n    timestamp: string;\n    uptime_seconds: number;\n    feeds: Array<{\n      id: string;\n      name: string;\n      status: string;\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      lastPollAt: string | null;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n      entityCount: number;\n      lastError: string | null;\n      avgLatencyMs: number;\n      errorCount24h: number;\n    }>;\n    alerts: {\n      count: number;\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      matchRate: number;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n      circuitOpen: boolean;\n      unmatchedCount: number;\n    };\n    delayDetector: {\n      enabled: boolean;\n      activePredictions: number;\n      lastRunAt: string | null;\n    };\n    delayPredictor: {\n      enabled: boolean;\n      modelAccuracy: number;\n      lastTrainedAt: string | null;\n    };\n    equipment: {\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      outages: number;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n    };\n    pushSubscriptions: number;\n    cacheHitRate: number;\n    memory: {\n      rssBytes: number;\n      heapUsedBytes: number;\n      heapTotalBytes: number;\n      externalBytes: number;\n    };\n    failingFeedsCount: number;\n  }): string {\n    const uptimeHours = Math.floor(healthData.uptime_seconds / 3600);\n    const uptimeMinutes = Math.floor((healthData.uptime_seconds % 3600) / 60);\n    const statusColor =\n      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n        ? \"#22c55e\"\n        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n          ? \"#ef4444\"\n          : \"#f59e0b\";\n    const statusIcon =\n      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n        ? \"✓\"\n        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n          ? \"✗\"\n          : \"⚠\";\n\n    return `<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n  <title>MTA My Way - System Status</title>\n  <style>\n    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }\n    body {\n      font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, \"Segoe UI\", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;\n      background: #0f172a;\n      color: #e2e8f0;\n      line-height: 1.5;\n      padding: 20px;\n    }\n    .container { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; }\n    h1 { font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 8px; }\n    .subtitle { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px; }\n    .status-banner {\n      display: inline-flex;\n      align-items: center;\n      gap: 8px;\n      padding: 12px 20px;\n      border-radius: 8px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      font-size: 16px;\n      margin-bottom: 24px;\n      background: ${statusColor}20;\n      border: 1px solid ${statusColor}40;\n    }\n    .status-icon { font-size: 20px; }\n    .status-text { color: ${statusColor}; }\n    .grid { display: grid; gap: 20px; }\n    .card {\n      background: #1e293b;\n      border-radius: 12px;\n      padding: 20px;\n      border: 1px solid #334155;\n    }\n    .card-title {\n      font-size: 16px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      margin-bottom: 16px;\n      color: #f1f5f9;\n    }\n    .metric-grid {\n      display: grid;\n      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr));\n      gap: 16px;\n    }\n    .metric { background: #0f172a; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; }\n    .metric-label { font-size: 12px; color: #94a3b8; margin-bottom: 4px; }\n    .metric-value { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; color: #f1f5f9; }\n    .metric-sub { font-size: 12px; color: #64748b; margin-top: 4px; }\n    .feed-table {\n      width: 100%;\n      border-collapse: collapse;\n      font-size: 14px;\n    }\n    .feed-table th {\n      text-align: left;\n      padding: 12px;\n      border-bottom: 1px solid #334155;\n      color: #94a3b8;\n      font-weight: 500;\n    }\n    .feed-table td {\n      padding: 12px;\n      border-bottom: 1px solid #1e293b;\n    }\n    .feed-table tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }\n    .status-badge {\n      display: inline-block;\n      padding: 4px 10px;\n      border-radius: 12px;\n      font-size: 12px;\n      font-weight: 500;\n    }\n    .status-ok { background: #22c55e20; color: #22c55e; }\n    .status-stale { background: #f59e0b20; color: #f59e0b; }\n    .status-circuit_open { background: #ef444420; color: #ef4444; }\n    .status-never_polled { background: #64748b20; color: #94a3b8; }\n    .last-updated {\n      text-align: center;\n      color: #64748b;\n      font-size: 12px;\n      margin-top: 32px;\n      padding-top: 16px;\n      border-top: 1px solid #1e293b;\n    }\n    @media <h1>MTA My Way</h1>\n    <p class=\"subtitle\">Real-time subway data system status</p>\n\n    <div class=\"status-banner\">\n      <span class=\"status-icon\">${statusIcon}</span>\n      <span class=\"status-text\">${healthData.status.toUpperCase()}</span>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"grid\">\n      <!-- System Overview -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">System Overview</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Uptime</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${uptimeHours}h ${uptimeMinutes}m</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Cache Hit Rate</div>\n            <div       <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.pushSubscriptions}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Failing Feeds</div>\n    <!-- Memory Usage -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Memory Usage</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div ${healthData.feeds\n              .map(\n                (feed) => `\n            <tr>\n              <td>${feed.name}</td>\n              <td><span class=\"status-badge status-${feed.status}\">${feed.status.replace(/_/g, \" \")}</span></td>\n              <td>${timeAgo(feed.lastSuccessAt)}</td>\n              <td>${feed.entityCount}</td>\n </div>\n\n      <!-- Alerts Status -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Service Alerts</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Active Alerts</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.alerts.count}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Match Rate</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${(healthData.alerts.matchRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.alerts.unmatchedCount} unmatched</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Last Update</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${timeAgo(healthData.alerts.lastSuccessAt)}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Status</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"circuit_open\" : \"ok\"}\">\n                ${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"Enabled\" : \"Disabled\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.delayDetector.activePredictions} active predictions</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Predictor</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.delayPredictor.enabled ? \"ok\" : \"never_polled\"}\">\n                ${healthData.delayPredictor.enabled ? \"Enabled\" : \"Disabled\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${(healthData.delayPredictor.modelAccuracy * 100).toFixed(0)}% accuracy</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Equipment</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.equipment.outages}</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">active outages</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"last-updated\">\n      Last updated: ${new Date(healthData.timestamp).toLocaleString()} •\n      <a href=\"/api/health\" style=\"color: #38bdf8;\">View JSON</a>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n</body>\n</html>`;\n  }\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Health endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  /**\n   * GET /api/health — Per-feed health status (JSON)\n   *\n   * Query validation: Uses `emptyQuerySchema` (`z.object({}).strict()`).\n   * Any query parameters at all cause a 400 with `{ error: \"validation failed\", details: [...] }`.\n   * No query parameters are accepted.\n   *\n   * Response top-level fields:\n   *   status              (\"ok\" | \"degraded\")\n   *     — \"ok\" when all feeds have circuitOpenAt===null AND lastSuccessAt!==null AND !isStale,\n   *       AND alerts has circuitOpen===false AND lastSuccessAt!==null.\n   *     — \"degraded\" otherwise.\n   *\n   *   timestamp           string (ISO 8601) — new Date().toISOString() at request time.\n   *\n   *   uptime_seconds      number — seconds since SERVER_START_MS (set at module load).\n   *\n   *   feeds               Array<FeedState> — one entry per GTFS-realtime feed.\n   *     Each element:\n   *       id                    string   — feed identifier (e.g. \"1\", \"26\", \"31\", \"36\").\n   *       name                  string   — human-readable feed name.\n   *       status                (\"circuit_open\" | \"never_polled\" | \"stale\" | \"ok\")\n   *         — \"circuit_open\" when circuitOpenAt !== null.\n   *         — \"never_polled\" when lastSuccessAt === null.\n   *         — \"stale\" when isStale === true.\n   *         — \"ok\" otherwise.\n   *       lastSuccessAt         string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful poll, or null.\n   *       lastPollAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last poll attempt, or null.\n   *       consecutiveFailures  number — count of consecutive failed polls.\n   *       entityCount           number — number of entities in the feed.\n   *       lastError             string | null — truncated (max 100 chars) last error message,\n   *                                  newlines stripped; null if no error.\n   *       tripReplacementPeriod string | null — the feed's trip replacement period.\n   *       avgLatencyMs          number — average of latencyHistory entries.\n   *       errorCount24h         number — count of error timestamps within the last 24 hours.\n   *       parseErrors           (feed-specific type) — parse error details for the feed.\n   *\n   *   alerts              object — alerts polling subsystem status.\n   *     count                  number  — number of alerts tracked.\n   *     lastSuccessAt          number | null — epoch-ms of last successful alert fetch, or null.\n   *     matchRate              number  — fraction of alerts that matched trips (0–1).\n   *     consecutiveFailures    number  — consecutive alert fetch failures.\n   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the alerts circuit breaker is open.\n   *     unmatchedCount         number  — alerts that did not match any known trip.\n   *\n   *   delayDetector       object — delay detector subsystem status.\n   *     trackedTrips           number — count of trips currently being tracked.\n   *     activeAlerts           number — count of active predicted delay alerts.\n   *     thresholdMultiplier    number — delay threshold configuration multiplier.\n   *     minTrainsForLineAlert  number — minimum trains needed to trigger a line-level alert.\n   *\n   *   delayPredictor     object — delay predictor subsystem status.\n   *     totalRecords           number — count of delay records stored.\n   *     aggregatedPatterns     number — count of aggregated statistical patterns.\n   *     minObservations        number — minimum observations required for a prediction.\n   *     currentWeather         WeatherCondition — current weather string (e.g. \"clear\").\n   *\n   *   equipment           object (EquipmentCacheStatus) — elevator/escalator outage status.\n   *     lastFetchAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last fetch attempt.\n   *     lastSuccessAt          string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful fetch.\n   *     outageCount            number — number of stations with tracked outages.\n   *     consecutiveFailures    number — consecutive equipment fetch failures.\n   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the equipment circuit breaker is open.\n   *\n   *   pushSubscriptions   number — count of active push notification subscriptions.\n   *\n   *   cacheHitRate        number — fraction 0–1, computed as cache_hits_total /\n   *                            (cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total) summed across all\n   *                            label combinations. Rounded to 2 decimal places.\n   *                            Returns 0 when there have been no cache requests.\n   *\n   *   memory             object — Node.js process memory snapshot via process.memoryUsage().\n   *     rssBytes               number — resident set size in bytes.\n   *     heapUsedBytes          number — active heap usage in bytes.\n   *     heapTotalBytes         number — total heap allocated in bytes.\n   *     externalBytes          number — external (C++/Buffer) memory in bytes.\n   *\n   *   failingFeedsCount  number — count of feeds where consecutiveFailures > 0 AND\n   *                            lastSuccessAt !== null AND time since last success > 5 minutes.\n   *\n   * HTTP status code:\n   *   200 — when fewer than UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD (3) feeds are failing\n   *         (consecutiveFailures > 0, had prior success, last success > 5 min ago).\n   *   503 — when 3 or more feeds meet the failing criteria above.\n   *   Note: \"degraded\" status can coexist with a 200 response (e.g. one stale feed\n   *         but fewer than 3 failing). 503 implies \"degraded\" but not vice versa.\n   */\n  app.get(\"/api/health\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n    );\n    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n\n    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n    const now = Date.now();\n    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n      (f) =>\n        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n    );\n    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n\n    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n\n    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n\n    // Get cache hit rate from metrics (sum across all label combinations)\n    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n    const cacheHitsMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_hits_total\");\n    const cacheMissesMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_misses_total\");\n    if (cacheHitsMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n    const cacheHitRate =\n      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? Math.round((cacheHitsValue / totalCacheRequests) * 100) / 100 : 0;\n\n    return c.json(\n      {\n        status,\n        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n        uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n        feeds: feedStates.map((f) => ({\n          id: f.id,\n          name: f.name,\n          status:\n            f.circuitOpenAt !== null\n              ? \"circuit_open\"\n              : f.lastSuccessAt === null\n                ? \"never_polled\"\n                : f.isStale\n                  ? \"stale\"\n                  : \"ok\",\n          lastSuccessAt: f.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(f.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n          lastPollAt: f.lastPollAt ? new Date(f.lastPollAt).toISOString() : null,\n          consecutiveFailures: f.consecutiveFailures,\n          entityCount: f.entityCount,\n          lastError: f.lastErrorMessage\n            ? f.lastErrorMessage.slice(0, 100).replace(/[\\r\\n]/g, \" \")\n            : null,\n          tripReplacementPeriod: f.tripReplacementPeriod,\n          avgLatencyMs: avgLatency(f.latencyHistory),\n          errorCount24h: errorCount24h(f.errorTimestamps),\n          parseErrors: f.parseErrors,\n        })),\n        alerts: {\n          count: alertsStatus.alertCount,\n          lastSuccessAt: alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt,\n          matchRate: alertsStatus.matchRate,\n          consecutiveFailures: alertsStatus.consecutiveFailures,\n          circuitOpen: alertsStatus.circuitOpen,\n          unmatchedCount: alertsStatus.unmatchedCount,\n        },\n        delayDetector: getDelayDetectorStatus(),\n        delayPredictor: getDelayPredictorStatus(),\n        equipment: getEquipmentStatus(),\n        pushSubscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n        cacheHitRate,\n        memory: {\n          rssBytes: memUsage.rss,\n          heapUsedBytes: memUsage.heapUsed,\n          heapTotalBytes: memUsage.heapTotal,\n          externalBytes: memUsage.external,\n        },\n        failingFeedsCount: failingFeeds.length,\n      },\n      httpStatus as 200 | 503\n    );\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Public status page (human-readable dashboard)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/status\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    // Fetch the same data as the health endpoint\n    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n    );\n    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n\n    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n    const now = Date.now();\n    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n      (f) =>\n        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n    );\n    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n\n    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n\n    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n\n    // Get cache hit rate from metrics\n    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n    const cacheHitsMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_hits_total\");\n    const cacheMissesMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_misses_total\");\n    if (cacheHitsMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n    const cacheHitRate =\n      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? Math.round((cacheHitsValue / totalCacheRequests) * 100) / 100 : 0;\n\n    const healthData = {\n      status,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n      uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n      feeds: feedStates.map((f) => ({\n        id: f.id,\n        name: f.name,\n        status:\n          f.circuitOpenAt !== null\n            ? \"circuit_open\"\n            : f.lastSuccessAt === null\n              ? \"never_polled\"\n              : f.isStale\n                ? \"stale\"\n                : \"ok\",\n        lastSuccessAt: f.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(f.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n        lastPollAt: f.lastPollAt ? new Date(f.lastPollAt).toISOString() : null,\n        consecutiveFailures: f.consecutiveFailures,\n        entityCount: f.entityCount,\n        lastError: f.lastErrorMessage\n          ? f.lastErrorMessage.slice(0, 100).replace(/[\\r\\n]/g, \" \")\n          : null,\n        avgLatencyMs: avgLatency(f.latencyHistory),\n        errorCount24h: errorCount24h(f.errorTimestamps),\n      })),\n      alerts: {\n        count: alertsStatus.alertCount,\n        lastSuccessAt: alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt,\n        matchRate: alertsStatus.matchRate,\n        consecutiveFailures: alertsStatus.consecutiveFailures,\n        circuitOpen: alertsStatus.circuitOpen,\n        unmatchedCount: alertsStatus.unmatchedCount,\n      },\n      delayDetector: getDelayDetectorStatus(),\n      delayPredictor: getDelayPredictorStatus(),\n      equipment: getEquipmentStatus(),\n      pushSubscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n      cacheHitRate,\n      memory: {\n        rssBytes: memUsage.rss,\n        heapUsedBytes: memUsage.heapUsed,\n        heapTotalBytes: memUsage.heapTotal,\n        externalBytes: memUsage.external,\n      },\n      failingFeedsCount: failingFeeds.length,\n    };\n\n    const html = buildStatusHtml(healthData);\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/html; charset=utf-8\");\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", \"public, max-age=30\");\n    return c.html(html, httpStatus as 200 | 503);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Metrics export endpoint (for Prometheus)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/metrics\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    const metricsText = metrics.exportPrometheus();\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.post(\"/api/security/csp-report\", async (c) => {\n    try {\n      const contentType = c.req.header(\"Content-Type\") ?? \"\";\n      const baseContentType = contentType.split(\";\")[0]!.trim().toLowerCase();\n      const allowed = [\"application/csp-report\", \"application/json\", \"application/reports+json\"];\n      if (!allowed.includes(baseContentType)) {\n        return c.json({ error: \"Unsupported Media Type\" }, 415);\n      }\n\n      const report = await c.req.json().catch(() => null);\n\n      if (!report) {\n        return c.json({ error: \"Invalid report\" }, 400);\n      }\n\n      // Log the CSP violation for security monitoring\n      logger.warn(\"CSP violation detected\", {\n        \"user-agent\": c.req.header(\"user-agent\"),\n        referrer: c.req.header(\"referrer\"),\n        \"x-forwarded-for\": c.req.header(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0] ?? \"unknown\",\n        report,\n      });\n\n      // Return 200 to acknowledge the report\n      return c.json({ received: true });\n    } catch (error) {\n      logger.error(\"Failed to process CSP report\", error as Error);\n      return c.json({ error: \"Failed to process report\" }, 400);\n    }\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Security disclosure policy (RFC 9116)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/.well-known/security.txt\", (c) => {\n    const contact = process.env[\"SECURITY_CONTACT\"] ?? \"mailto:security@jedarden.com\";\n    const body =\n      `Contact: ${contact}\\n` +\n      `Preferred-Languages: en\\n` +\n      `Policy: https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way/security/policy\\n`;\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; charset=utf-8\");\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", \"max-age=86400\");\n    return c.text(body);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Real-time arrivals\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/arrivals/:id\", (c) => {\n    const params = validateParams(c, stationIdParamsSchema);\n    if (params   equipment: equipmentSummary?.equipment ??
